info.name: Atlassian pullrequests/{selected user}/
info.description:
Needs description.

Contact Support:
 Name: Bitbucket Support
 Email: support@bitbucket.org

item.name: Atlassian List Pull Requests For A User

query.description: Only return pull requests that are in this state. This parameter can be repeated.

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the username of the pull request author, the author's UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{account UUID}`, or the author's Atlassian ID.

request.description:
Returns all pull requests authored by the specified user.

By default only open pull requests are returned. This can be controlled
using the `state` query parameter. To retrieve pull requests that are
in one of multiple states, repeat the `state` parameter for each
individual state.

This endpoint also supports filtering and sorting of the results. See
[filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering) for more details.

response.name: All pull requests authored by the specified user.

query.description: Only return pull requests that are in this state. This parameter can be repeated.

response.name: If the specified user does not exist.

query.description: Only return pull requests that are in this state. This parameter can be repeated.

item.name: Atlassian List A Pull Request Activity Log

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of the pull request's activity log.

This handler serves both a v20 and internal endpoint. The v20 endpoint
returns reviewer comments, updates, approvals and request changes. The internal
endpoint includes those plus tasks and attachments.

Comments created on a file or a line of code have an inline property.

Comment example:
```
{
"pagelen": 20,
"values": [
{
"comment": {
"links": {
"self": {
"href": "https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/repositories/atlassian/atlaskit-mk-2/pullrequests/5695/comments/118571088"
},
"html": {

}
},
"deleted": false,
"pullrequest": {

"id": 5695,

},

}
},
"title": "username/NONE: small change from onFocus to onClick to handle tabbing through the page and not expand the editor unless a click event triggers it"
},
"content": {
"raw": "inline with to a dn from lines",
"markup": "markdown",
"html": "inline with to a dn from lines",

},
```

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.

request.description:
Redact the authenticated user's approval of the specified pull
request.

response.name: An empty response indicating the authenticated user's approval has been withdrawn.

surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.

surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.

response.name: The comment is reopened.

item.name: Atlassian Delete A Comment On A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.
(Required) The id of the comment.

request.description: Deletes a specific pull request comment.

response.name: Successful deletion.
If the authenticated user does not have access to delete the comment.
If the comment does not exist.

item.name: Atlassian Get A Comment On A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.
(Required) The id of the comment.

request.description: Returns a specific pull request comment.

response.name: The comment.
If the authenticated user does not have access to the pull request.
If the comment does not exist.

item.name: Atlassian Update A Comment On A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.
(Required) The id of the comment.

request.description: Updates a specific pull request comment.

response.name: The updated comment.
If the authenticated user does not have access to the comment.
If the comment does not exist.

item.name: Atlassian List Comments On A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of the pull request's comments.

This includes both global, inline comments and replies.

The default sorting is oldest to newest and can be overridden with
the `sort` query parameter.

This endpoint also supports filtering and sorting of the results. See
[filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering) for more
details.

response.name: A paginated list of comments made on the given pull request, in chronological order.
If the authenticated user does not have access to the pull request.
If the pull request does not exist.

item.name: Atlassian Create A Comment On A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.

request.description:
Creates a new pull request comment.

Returns the newly created pull request comment.

response.name: The newly created comment.

description.content: The URL of the new comment

response.name: If the authenticated user does not have access to the pull request.
If the pull request does not exist.

item.name: Atlassian List Commits On A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.

item.name: Atlassian Get The Merge Task Status For A Pull Request

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.
(Required) ID of the merge task

request.description:
When merging a pull request takes too long, the client receives a
task ID along with a 202 status code. The task ID can be used in a call
to this endpoint to check the status of a merge task.

```
curl -X GET https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/repositories/atlassian/bitbucket/pullrequests/2286/merge/task-status/
```

If the merge task is not yet finished, a PENDING status will be returned.

```
HTTP/2 200
{
"task_status": "PENDING",
"links": {
"self": {
"href": "https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/repositories/atlassian/bitbucket/pullrequests/2286/merge/task-status/"
}
}
}
```

If the merge was successful, a SUCCESS status will be returned.

```
HTTP/2 200
{
"task_status": "SUCCESS",
"links": {
"self": {
"href": "https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/repositories/atlassian/bitbucket/pullrequests/2286/merge/task-status/"
}
},
"merge_result":
}
```

If the merge task failed, an error will be returned.

```
{
"type": "error",
"error": {
"message": ""
}
}
```

response.name: Returns a task status if the merge is either pending or successful, and if it is successful, a pull request
If the provided task ID does not relate to this pull request, or if something went wrong during the merge operation
The user making the request does not have permission to the repo and is different from the user who queued the task

query.description:
Default value is false.


When set to true, runs merge asynchronously and
immediately returns a 202 with polling link to
the task-status API in the Location header.


When set to false, runs merge and waits for it to
complete, returning 200 when it succeeds. If the
duration of the merge exceeds a timeout threshold,
the API returns a 202 with polling link to the
task-status API in the Location header.

variable.description:

surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.
(Required) The id of the pull request.

request.description: Merges the pull request.

response.name: The pull request object.

query.description:

response.name: In the Location header, the URL to poll for the pull request merge status

query.description:

response.name:
If the merge took too long and timed out.
In this case the caller should retry the request later

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,

request.description:
Returns all pull requests on the specified repository.

By default only open pull requests are returned. This can be controlled
using the `state` query parameter. To retrieve pull requests that are
in one of multiple states, repeat the `state` parameter for each
individual state.

This endpoint also supports filtering and sorting of the results. See
[filtering and sorting](/cloud/bitbucket/rest/intro/#filtering) for more details.

response.name: All pull requests on the specified repository.

query.description: Only return pull requests that are in this state. This parameter can be repeated.

response.name: If the repository is private and the request was not authenticated.

query.description: Only return pull requests that are in this state. This parameter can be repeated.

response.name: If the specified repository does not exist.

query.description: Only return pull requests that are in this state. This parameter can be repeated.

variable.description:
(Required) This can either be the workspace ID (slug) or the workspace UUID
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{workspace UUID}`.

(Required) This can either be the repository slug or the UUID of the repository,
surrounded by curly-braces, for example: `{repository UUID}`.

request.description:
Creates a new pull request where the destination repository is
this repository and the author is the authenticated user.

The minimum required fields to create a pull request are `title` and
`source`, specified by a branch name.

```
curl https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0/repositories/my-workspace/my-repository/pullrequests \
-u my-username:my-password \
--request POST \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data '{
"title": "My Title",
"source": {
"branch": {
"name": "staging"
}
}
}'
```

If the pull request's `destination` is not specified, it will default
to the `repository.mainbranch`. To open a pull request to a
different branch, say from a feature branch to a staging branch,
specify a `destination` (same format as the `source`):

```
{
"title": "My Title",
"source": {
"branch": {
"name": "my-feature-branch"
}
},
"destination": {
"branch": {
"name": "staging"
}
}
}
```

Reviewers can be specified by adding an array of user objects as the
`reviewers` property.

```
{
"title": "My Title",
"source": {
"branch": {
"name": "my-feature-branch"
}
},
"reviewers": [
{
"uuid": "{504c3b62-8120-4f0c-a7bc-87800b9d6f70}"
}
]
}
```

Other fields:

* `description` - a string
* `close_source_branch` - boolean that specifies if the source branch should be closed upon merging

response.name: The newly created pull request.

description.content: The URL of new newly created pull request.

response.name: If the input document was invalid, or if the caller lacks the privilege to create repositories under the targeted account.
If the request was not authenticated.
